content_en:
Bilal, the Prophet's Muezien led Safiyya and a cousin of hers and made them cross the scene of fighting in which there were many Jewish deaths. Safiyya kept patient and did not worry or cry. Unlike Safiyya, her cousin cried, slapped her cheeks and put earth over her head.

They were brought before the Prophet (pbuh). When the Prophet (pbuh) noticed the impatient state of Safiyya's cousin which was manifested in her torn dress, dusty face and cries, he said, "Take this devil away!" Then he came close to Safiyya and looked kindly to her. Then he said, "Did mercy forsake you Bilal to lead two women through the deaths of their men."

Then, the Prophet turned to Safiyya and covered her with his garment and kept her behind him. This was an indication that the Prophet (pbuh) chose her for himself. Muslims did not know, by then, whether the Prophet married her or adopted her as a slave girl? But after he veiled her, they realized that the married her.

In a prophetic hadith narrated by Anas (may Allah be pleased with him) that when the Messenger of Allah (pbuh) took Safiyya bint Huyah, he said to her, "Do you like me?" She said, "O Messenger of Allah when I was an atheist I wished that. Then how would I be after I had embraced Islam?"

After her waiting period had expired (to verify that she is not pregnant) the Prophet married her and made her dowry freeing her. The prophet (pbuh) waited for a period of time in Khaybar until they reposed, he took Safiyya behind him after she became familiar with the new position.

The Prophet encamped with his army six miles away from Khaybar. He wanted to sleep with her but she refused. Thereby, he became angry of her refusal.

The prophet (pbuh) went on walking towards Madina followed by his army. When he reached as-Sahba’ (a place) he encamped there to have a rest. He noticed Safiyya ready for consummating the marriage. Then Umm Ana Ibn Malik (may Allah be pleased with her) came to comb, adorn and perfume her until she became so charming. Umm Sinan commented, “I never saw a bright woman like her." The wedding banquet was offered in which the people ate of Khayber food: dates, oil, milk, until they became satiated.

When the Prophet (pbuh) was alone with her, he was angry with due to her previous refusal. She (the bride) lovingly told him an amazing tiding. She said, "I saw in a dream when I got married to Kinanah ibn ar-Rabee’ the moon falling upon my knee. When I got up I narrated the dream to Kinanah. He got angry and said, "Would you like the King of Hijaz, Muhammad?" Then He slapped my face. The trace of this punch was still in her face. The Prophet (pbuh) smiled and then asked, "Why did you refuse me at the first time?" She, at once, answered, "I feared the Jews who might be nearby" then the Prophet's face became bright and he consummated his marriage with her. She was seventeen by then.

Narrated Anas (may Allah be pleased upon him), "The Prophet (pbuh) stayed for three days at a place between Khaybar and Madina, and __ there he consummated his marriage with Safiyya bint Huiyay. I invited the Muslims to a banquet, which included neither meat nor bread. The Prophet (pbuh) ordered for leather dining sheets to be spread and then the dates, dreiet yoghourt and butter were provided over it,

and that was the banquet (walima) of the Prophet (pbuh). The Muslims asked whether Safiyya would be considered as his wife or as a slave girl of what his right hand possessed. Then they said, "If the Prophet (pbuh) screens her from the the people, then she iss the Prophet's wife but if he does not screen her, then she is a slave girl." So when the Prophet (pbuh) proceeded he made a place for her (on the camel) behind him and

screened her from people." [Reported by Al-Bukhari]

Reaching Madina, the Prophet (pbuh) did not let her stay with his other wives. Rrather he preferd that she stay in the house of his companion, Harithah ibn an-Nu‘ man.

When the women in Madina knew her coming, they looked forward to seeing her. The Prophet (pbuh) noticed ‘Aisha hiding herself among the women in order to see her. Although she was veiled, the Prophet identified her, held her dress and asked her, "What did you see, O Shuqaira' (blond woman)?" She out of jealousy answered, "She is just a Jewish woman." The prophet (pbuh) replied, "Do not say that; she embraced Islam."

When Safiyya moved to the Prophet's house, the other Prophet's wives became jealous of her beauty. Therefore, they boasted over her about their Arabic origin while she was foreigner.

Once, she was informed that Hafsa said that she was a daughter of a Jew and that made her weep. When the prophet (pbuh) found her weeping, he asked, "Why do you weep?" She replied, "Hafsa said that I am the daughter of a Jew." Then the Prophet (pbuh) said, "You are the daughter of a Prophet, the niece of a Prophet and the wife of a Prophet. Thus, how could she boast over you?" Then, he said, "Fear Allah, Hafsa."

Such words pleased her. Whenever any one boasted herself over her, she often said: "Why, I am Muhammad's wife, Harun's daughter and Musa's niece."

It is related that she had a bondswoman during the caliphate of ‘Umar who went to him and said, "Safiyya loves Saturday and has connections with the Jews. Whereupon ‘Umar sent for her and investigated the case. She replied, "As for Saturday, I have not loved it since Allah gave me Friday in its place, and as for the Jews, I have kinfolk among them and maintain my family ties." When she asked her servant what made her do what that, she was told, "The Devil," to which Safiyya responded, "You may go now, you are free.”

She died in Madianh in 50 AH - 670 AC during the caliphate of Mu’ awyah. She was buried in Bagee' beside the other Mothers of the Believers (may Allah be pleased with them all)

She was Juwairiya bint al-Harith ibn Abu Dirar ibn Labib alKhuza'iyya, who was from Mustalaq. She was a beautiful woman. She was taken as a prisoner of war when Muslims defeated the tribe of Banu al-Mustalaq in the battle of Al-Muraysee’.

When the spoils of the war were distributed among the warriors, Juwairiya was in the share of Thabit ibn Qays ibn ash-Shammas or a cousin to him. She was twenty by then. She tried to escape such slavery and made a contract with Thabit ibn Qays to free her in return for money. Then she went to the Prophet (pbuh) asking for help; to free herself. The Prophet (pbuh) sympathized with her and said, "Would you want something better than that?" She anxiously said, "What is this, Messenger of Allah he said, "I will pay your contract and marry you" she was so happy that she could not believe what she heard. She pleasantly agreed. Then, the Prophet (pbuh) said, "Ok".

‘Aisha narrated, "Then the people knew that the Prophet (pbuh) got married to Juwairiya bint al-Harith ibn Abu Dirar. Thereupon, they freed whom they had captured of her tribe because they became relatives by marriage. They freed about a hundred of Banu alMustalaq families. I have never known a woman who blessed her tribe greater than her."

‘Aisha, the mother of the believers added, "She (Juwayriya) had a good looking and captivated whomever saw her.” When she came to the Prophet (pbuh) to help her pay the contract of freedom. By Allah, no sooner did 1 see her standing by my door than I grew jealous and expected that the Prophet (pbuh) world realize what I realized."

Afterwards, the bride of Banu al-Mustalaq joined the Prophet's house. She was called Burrah before the Prophet (pbuh) changed her name to Juwairiya.

Ibn Hajar stated in "Al-Isabah" that Juwairiya was a true faithful. Once her father came to the Prophet (pbuh) complaining about the capture of his daughter. Then the Prophet said, "Would you like to give her the choice?" He said, "Ok." Accordingly, her father offered her to return, she said, I have chosen Allah and His Messenger."

Ibn Hisham also narrated that her father, al-Harith, two of his sons and some people of his tribe embraced Islam. She (may Allah be pleased with her) died in 50 or 56 AH may Allah have mercy on her whose marriage was a blessing for her tribe. After the Prophet (pbuh) had married her, her people converted to Islam. This is of course one of the wisdoms of polygamy in Islam.

She was Maymuna bint al-Hurith ibn Hazan ibn Bujair, ibn alHazm, ibn Ruwaiba ibn ‘Abdullah, ibn Hilal, ibn ‘Amer, ibn Sa’‘sa‘a alHilaliyya. She was a sister of Umm al-Fadl, Al-Abbas' wife, an aunt of Khalid ibn al-Walid and ibn Abbas (may Allah be pleased with them all). She was among the mistresses of Quraish who were famous of honour and nobility.

She first got married to Mas‘ud ibn Amr ath-Thagafi before Islam. Nevertheless, she frequently visited her sister, Umm al-Fadl; from her she leamt some teachings of Islam and the news of the Islamic victory in Badr and Uhud that affected her heart.

When the Muslims retumed victorious after the battle of Khaybar, she was in her sister's house. She was so happy because of the victory. When she went home, she found her husband depressed and sad. They quarreled and then separated each other. She resorted to Al-Abbas' house.

When the Prophet (pbuh) and the Muslims came to stay in Makka for three days to perform their rites as stated in the Hudaybya truce, they entered Makka peacefully on the appinted day. Then they loudly chanted "labbayka allahumma labayk. Labbayka la shareeka lake Jabbyaka" (Ever at Your service, O Allah, ever at Your service. Ever at Your service. You have no partner, ever at Your service." All parts of Makka responded to this clamour and the land beneath the disbeliever's feet quivered. They resorted to mountains and hills so as not to see Muhammad and Muslims' returning to Makka strong. Some men and women of those who did not declare thier faith publicly stayed in Makka believing that the day of victory was coming. Maymuna was among those who did not declare their faith, However she was not satisfied with her hidden belief. Therefore, she declared publicly that she embraced Islam. Furthermore, she wanted to declare her wish to join the Prophet's house. She talked to her sister Umm al-Fadl about her wish. In turn, her sister told her husband Al-Abbas and commissioned him to her marriage. Then Al-Abbas went at once to the Prophet (pbuh)
content_id:
Bilal, muazin Nabi, memimpin Shafiyyah dan sepupunya serta membawa mereka melewati medan pertempuran yang di dalamnya terdapat banyak kematian orang-orang Yahudi. Shafiyyah tetap sabar dan tidak khawatir atau menangis. Berbeda dengan Shafiyyah, sepupunya menangis, menampar pipinya, dan menaburkan tanah di kepalanya.

Mereka dibawa menghadap Nabi (SAW). Ketika Nabi (SAW) memperhatikan kondisi sepupu Shafiyyah yang tidak sabar yang ditunjukkan dalam pakaiannya yang robek, wajah berdebu, dan tangisannya, beliau bersabda, 'Singkirkan setan ini!' Kemudian beliau mendekati Shafiyyah dan memandangnya dengan penuh kasih. Kemudian beliau bersabda, 'Apakah belas kasihan meninggalkanmu, wahai Bilal, sehingga kamu membawa dua wanita melewati kematian laki-laki mereka?'

Kemudian, Nabi berpaling kepada Shafiyyah dan menyelimutinya dengan jubahnya serta menjaganya di belakangnya. Ini adalah isyarat bahwa Nabi (SAW) memilih dia untuk dirinya sendiri. Kaum Muslimin belum tahu saat itu apakah Nabi menikahinya atau menjadikannya sebagai budak? Tetapi setelah beliau menyelimutinya, mereka menyadari bahwa beliau menikahinya.

Dalam sebuah hadis Nabi yang diriwayatkan Anas (ra) bahwa ketika Rasulullah (SAW) membawa Shafiyyah binti Huyay, beliau berkata kepadanya, 'Apakah kamu menyukaiku?' Dia berkata, 'Wahai Rasulullah, ketika aku masih kafir aku menginginkan itu. Lalu bagaimana mungkin aku tidak menyukainya setelah aku memeluk Islam?'

Setelah masa iddahnya berakhir (untuk memastikan bahwa dia tidak hamil), Nabi menikahinya dan menjadikan pembebasannya sebagai mas kawinnya. Nabi (SAW) menunggu beberapa saat di Khaibar hingga mereka beristirahat, lalu beliau membawa Shafiyyah di belakangnya setelah dia terbiasa dengan posisi barunya.

Nabi berkemah bersama pasukannya enam mil dari Khaibar. Beliau ingin tidur bersamanya tetapi dia menolak. Karena itu, beliau menjadi marah dengan penolakannya.

Nabi (SAW) terus berjalan menuju Madinah diikuti oleh pasukannya. Ketika mencapai As-Shahba (sebuah tempat), beliau berkemah untuk beristirahat. Beliau melihat Shafiyyah siap untuk menyempurnakan pernikahan. Kemudian Umm Anas Ibn Malik (ra) datang untuk menyisir, mendandani, dan mewangikannya hingga dia menjadi sangat memukau. Umm Sinan berkomentar, 'Aku tidak pernah melihat wanita secemerlang dia.' Jamuan pernikahan disajikan di mana orang-orang memakan makanan Khaibar: kurma, minyak, susu, hingga mereka kenyang.

Ketika Nabi (SAW) berduaan dengannya, beliau marah padanya karena penolakannya sebelumnya. Dia (sang pengantin) dengan penuh kasih menceritakan kabar yang menakjubkan kepadanya. Dia berkata, 'Aku bermimpi ketika aku menikahi Kinanah ibn Ar-Rabi', bulan jatuh ke atas lututku. Ketika aku bangun, aku menceritakan mimpi itu kepada Kinanah. Dia marah dan berkata, 'Apakah kamu menginginkan Raja Hijaz, Muhammad?' Kemudian dia menampar wajahku.' Bekas tamparan itu masih ada di wajahnya. Nabi (SAW) tersenyum dan kemudian bertanya, 'Mengapa kamu menolakku pertama kali?' Dia langsung menjawab, 'Aku takut orang-orang Yahudi mungkin berada di dekat sini.' Kemudian wajah Nabi menjadi cerah dan beliau menyempurnakan pernikahannya dengannya. Dia berusia tujuh belas tahun saat itu.

Anas (ra) meriwayatkan, 'Nabi (SAW) tinggal selama tiga hari di sebuah tempat antara Khaibar dan Madinah, dan di sana beliau menyempurnakan pernikahannya dengan Shafiyyah binti Huyay. Aku mengundang kaum Muslimin ke pesta pernikahan yang tidak ada daging maupun roti. Nabi (SAW) memerintahkan untuk menggelar alas makan dari kulit kemudian disediakan kurma, yogurt kering, dan mentega di atasnya, dan itulah pesta pernikahan (walimah) Nabi (SAW). Kaum Muslimin bertanya apakah Shafiyyah akan dianggap sebagai istrinya atau sebagai budak yang dimiliki oleh tangan kanannya. Kemudian mereka berkata, 'Jika Nabi (SAW) menyelimutinya dari orang-orang, maka dia adalah istri Nabi, tetapi jika beliau tidak menyelimutinya, maka dia adalah budak.' Jadi ketika Nabi (SAW) berangkat, beliau membuat tempat untuknya (di atas unta) di belakang beliau dan menyelimutinya dari orang-orang.' (Diriwayatkan oleh Imam Al-Bukhari)

Tiba di Madinah, Nabi (SAW) tidak membiarkannya tinggal bersama istri-istrinya yang lain. Beliau lebih memilih agar dia tinggal di rumah sahabatnya, Haritsah ibn An-Nu'man.

Ketika para wanita di Madinah mengetahui kedatangannya, mereka menantikan untuk melihatnya. Nabi (SAW) memperhatikan 'Aisyah yang menyamarkan diri di antara para wanita untuk melihatnya. Meskipun dia bercadar, Nabi mengenalinya, memegang bajunya, dan bertanya kepadanya, 'Apa yang kamu lihat, wahai Shuqaira (wanita berambut pirang)?' Dia menjawab karena cemburu, 'Dia hanyalah seorang wanita Yahudi.' Nabi (SAW) menjawab, 'Jangan berkata begitu; dia telah memeluk Islam.'

Ketika Shafiyyah pindah ke rumah Nabi, para istri Nabi yang lain menjadi cemburu dengan kecantikannya. Oleh karena itu, mereka membanggakan diri atas asal-usul Arab mereka sementara dia adalah orang asing.

Suatu ketika, dia diberitahu bahwa Hafshah berkata bahwa dia adalah putri seorang Yahudi dan itu membuatnya menangis. Ketika Nabi (SAW) mendapatinya menangis, beliau bertanya, 'Mengapa kamu menangis?' Dia menjawab, 'Hafshah berkata bahwa aku adalah putri seorang Yahudi.' Kemudian Nabi (SAW) bersabda, 'Engkau adalah putri seorang Nabi, keponakan seorang Nabi, dan istri seorang Nabi. Jadi bagaimana dia bisa membanggakan diri atasmu?' Kemudian beliau bersabda, 'Bertakwalah kepada Allah, Hafshah.'

Kata-kata itu menyenangkannya. Setiap kali seseorang membanggakan diri atasnya, dia sering berkata, 'Mengapa? Aku adalah istri Muhammad, putri Harun, dan keponakan Musa.'

Diceritakan bahwa dia memiliki seorang budak perempuan di masa kekhalifahan 'Umar yang pergi menemuinya dan berkata, 'Shafiyyah menyukai hari Sabtu dan memiliki hubungan dengan orang-orang Yahudi.' Maka 'Umar mengutus orang untuk memanggilnya dan menyelidiki kasusnya. Dia menjawab, 'Adapun hari Sabtu, aku sudah tidak menyukainya sejak Allah memberiku hari Jumat sebagai gantinya, dan adapun orang-orang Yahudi, aku memiliki keluarga di antara mereka dan menjaga silaturahmi.' Ketika dia menanyai pelayannya apa yang membuatnya melakukan seperti itu, pelayannya berkata, 'Setan.' Shafiyyah pun menjawab, 'Pergilah sekarang, kamu merdeka.'

Dia meninggal di Madinah pada tahun 50 H / 670 M pada masa kekhalifahan Mu'awiyah. Dia dimakamkan di Baqi' di samping para Ibu Orang-Orang Beriman yang lain (semoga Allah meridhai mereka semua).

Dia adalah Juwairiyah binti Al-Harits ibn Abu Dirar ibn Labib Al-Khuza'iyyah, yang berasal dari Mustalaq. Dia adalah wanita yang cantik. Dia ditawan sebagai tawanan perang ketika kaum Muslimin mengalahkan suku Banu Al-Mustalaq dalam Perang Al-Muraysyi'.

Ketika rampasan perang dibagikan di antara para pejuang, Juwairiyah mendapatkan bagian Tsabit ibn Qays ibn Asy-Syammas atau sepupunya. Dia berusia dua puluh tahun saat itu. Dia berusaha melepaskan diri dari perbudakan tersebut dan membuat perjanjian dengan Tsabit ibn Qays untuk membebaskannya dengan imbalan sejumlah uang. Kemudian dia pergi menemui Nabi (SAW) meminta pertolongan untuk membebaskan dirinya. Nabi (SAW) bersimpati kepadanya dan berkata, 'Apakah kamu menginginkan sesuatu yang lebih baik dari itu?' Dia dengan penuh harap berkata, 'Apa itu, Rasulullah?' Beliau bersabda, 'Aku akan membayar perjanjianmu dan menikahinya.' Dia begitu bahagia sehingga tidak bisa mempercayai apa yang didengarnya. Dia dengan senang hati setuju. Kemudian, Nabi (SAW) bersabda, 'Baiklah.'

'Aisyah meriwayatkan, 'Kemudian orang-orang tahu bahwa Nabi (SAW) menikahi Juwairiyah binti Al-Harits ibn Abu Dirar. Maka, mereka membebaskan siapa pun yang mereka tawan dari sukunya karena mereka menjadi kerabat melalui pernikahan. Mereka membebaskan sekitar seratus keluarga dari Banu Al-Mustalaq. Aku tidak pernah tahu seorang wanita yang lebih banyak memberkahi sukunya daripada dia.'

'Aisyah, ibu orang-orang beriman, menambahkan, 'Dia (Juwayriyah) memiliki penampilan yang menarik dan memikat siapa pun yang melihatnya.' Ketika dia datang menemui Nabi (SAW) untuk meminta bantuan membayar perjanjian kebebasan. Demi Allah, begitu aku melihatnya berdiri di depan pintuku, aku menjadi cemburu dan menduga bahwa Nabi (SAW) akan menyadari apa yang kusadari.'

Setelah itu, pengantin Banu Al-Mustalaq bergabung dengan rumah Nabi. Dia dipanggil Burrah sebelum Nabi (SAW) mengubah namanya menjadi Juwairiyah.

Ibn Hajar menyatakan dalam 'Al-Ishabah' bahwa Juwairiyah adalah mukminah yang sejati. Suatu ketika ayahnya datang menemui Nabi (SAW) mengadu tentang penangkapan putrinya. Kemudian Nabi bersabda, 'Apakah kamu ingin memberikan pilihan kepadanya?' Dia berkata, 'Baiklah.' Maka, ayahnya menawarkan kepadanya untuk kembali; dia berkata, 'Aku telah memilih Allah dan Rasul-Nya.'

Ibn Hisyam juga meriwayatkan bahwa ayahnya, Al-Harits, dua putranya, dan beberapa orang dari sukunya memeluk Islam. Dia (ra) meninggal pada tahun 50 atau 56 H. Semoga Allah merahmatinya yang pernikahannya menjadi berkah bagi sukunya. Setelah Nabi (SAW) menikahinya, kaumnya memeluk Islam. Ini tentu saja merupakan salah satu hikmah poligami dalam Islam.

Dia adalah Maimunah binti Al-Harits ibn Hazan ibn Bujair, ibn Al-Hazm, ibn Ruwaibah ibn 'Abdullah, ibn Hilal, ibn 'Amir, ibn Sa'sa'ah Al-Hilaliyyah. Dia adalah saudara perempuan Umm Al-Fadhl, istri Al-Abbas, bibi Khalid ibn Al-Walid dan Ibn Abbas (ra). Dia termasuk di antara para nyonya Quraisy yang terkenal dengan kehormatan dan kemuliaan.

Pertama kali dia menikah dengan Mas'ud ibn 'Amr Ats-Tsaqafi sebelum Islam. Meskipun demikian, dia sering mengunjungi saudarinya, Umm Al-Fadhl; dari saudarinya dia belajar beberapa ajaran Islam dan kabar kemenangan Islam di Badar dan Uhud yang mempengaruhi hatinya.

Ketika kaum Muslimin kembali sebagai pemenang setelah Perang Khaibar, dia berada di rumah saudarinya. Dia sangat gembira karena kemenangan itu. Ketika dia pulang ke rumah, dia mendapati suaminya dalam keadaan sedih dan murung. Mereka bertengkar dan kemudian berpisah satu sama lain. Dia pun berpindah ke rumah Al-Abbas.

Ketika Nabi (SAW) dan kaum Muslimin datang untuk tinggal di Makkah selama tiga hari untuk menunaikan manasik sebagaimana dinyatakan dalam perjanjian Hudaibiyah, mereka memasuki Makkah dengan damai pada hari yang ditentukan. Kemudian mereka dengan keras berseru 'Labbaika Allahumma labbaik. Labbaika laa syarika laka labbaik.' Semua penjuru Makkah bergema dengan seruan ini dan tanah di bawah kaki kaum kafir gemetar. Mereka berlindung ke gunung-gunung dan perbukitan agar tidak melihat Muhammad dan kaum Muslimin kembali ke Makkah dalam keadaan kuat. Beberapa laki-laki dan perempuan dari mereka yang tidak menyatakan keimanan mereka secara terbuka tetap tinggal di Makkah sambil percaya bahwa hari kemenangan akan datang. Maimunah adalah salah satu dari mereka yang tidak menyatakan keimanannya. Namun dia tidak puas dengan keimanannya yang tersembunyi. Oleh karena itu, dia secara terbuka menyatakan bahwa dia memeluk Islam. Selain itu, dia ingin menyatakan keinginannya untuk bergabung dengan rumah Nabi. Dia berbicara kepada saudarinya Umm Al-Fadhl tentang keinginannya. Saudarinya kemudian memberitahu suaminya Al-Abbas dan mempercayakannya untuk pernikahan itu. Kemudian Al-Abbas langsung pergi menemui Nabi (SAW).
